meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Master SaaS Pricing: Overcome No Testing Knowledge (Tutorial)
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between coding and business. Learn to implement professional SaaS pricing models even without testing experience using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: You don't need a QA degree to launch. By decoupling your pricing logic into a dedicated service layer and using Claude Code to generate unit tests based on your business constraints, you ensure reliability. This automated verification allows you to iterate on complex SaaS pricing structures with total confidence.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a pricing model implementation is where most student projects die. You have the features, you have the stack, but the paralyzing fear of 'breaking the billing'—especially without a background in software testing—keeps your app in local development forever. It is the invisible wall between a side project and a real-world SaaS.

We are going to shatter that wall. By leveraging modern AI-assisted engineering, we transform testing from a daunting chore into an automated safety net. You aren't just writing code; you are building a resilient revenue engine that validates itself. Let's turn your lack of testing knowledge into a competitive advantage.

steps.title: Define Your Pricing Logic
steps.description: Isolate your pricing logic into a pure function to make it easier to test without external dependencies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a clean, modular TypeScript function for a SaaS pricing engine that accepts user tier and usage metrics as inputs and returns a calculated monthly cost.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing.ts file with a calculatePrice function handling Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers.'

steps.title: Generate Automated Test Specs
steps.description: Use AI to define edge cases for your pricing logic, such as zero-usage or negative billing scenarios.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a list of 10 edge-case scenarios for a SaaS pricing model including boundary tests for usage limits and tier transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a Vitest test suite for pricing.ts that covers all edge cases identified in the requirements.'

steps.title: Implement Testing Safety Rails
steps.description: Establish a testing environment that runs automatically every time you modify your pricing code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a CI-ready testing script that validates the pricing engine against the previously defined edge cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup vitest configuration and add a test script to package.json.'

steps.title: Simulate Revenue Scenarios
steps.description: Validate that your logic holds up under heavy, simulated user load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a simulation script that generates 1000 random user usage profiles and verifies the pricing output against expected ranges.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a benchmark test that runs the calculatePrice function across 1000 randomized inputs.'

steps.title: Deploy with Confidence
steps.description: Finalize your pipeline so that no pricing change can be pushed to production without passing tests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a deployment hook that runs the test suite and blocks deployment if any pricing logic fails.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add a pre-commit hook that runs npm test to ensure no pricing regressions are introduced.'

pro_tips: Treat pricing as a pure function; never mix database calls with your pricing math to keep tests fast and deterministic.
Use 'Property-Based Testing' where the AI generates thousands of inputs to find the one specific value that breaks your code.
Always log the 'reasoning' behind a price change in your database; it helps debug customer complaints faster than re-running code.
Keep your test suite under 2 seconds; if it takes longer, you won't run it often enough during development.
Document your pricing edge cases in a README.md; future-you will need to know why you decided that 'zero usage' counts as a full month.
content.next_steps: Integrate your validated pricing engine with a real payment gateway like Stripe, using the same test-first methodology to verify your webhook handlers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build professional-grade SaaS tools without the fear of technical failure. Your portfolio is about to look like it was built by a senior engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
